report_name: Ransomware Attack on North Dakota Water Treatment Plant Forces 16-Hour Manual Operation

threat_actors.name: Minot Water Plant Attacker
threat_actors.description: An unknown threat actor responsible for the March 2026 ransomware attack against the Minot, North Dakota water treatment plant's SCADA system.
threat_actors.goals: Disrupting critical infrastructure operations and potential financial extortion via ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware was deployed on the SCADA server, encrypting files and disrupting digital monitoring and control operations.

attack_patterns.name: Loss of View
attack_patterns.description: By encrypting the SCADA system, the attackers directly inhibited the operators' ability to monitor and respond to plant conditions digitally, forcing a switch to manual operations.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely used vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies to prevent easy restoration of the encrypted SCADA server, a common ransomware tactic.

tools.name: vssadmin.exe
tools.description: The Volume Shadow Copy Service administrative command-line tool was likely used by the attackers to delete system backups and inhibit recovery.

identities.name: Minot Water Treatment Plant
identities.description: A water treatment facility in Minot, North Dakota, whose SCADA system was compromised by a ransomware attack, forcing a switch to manual operations for 16 hours.

indicators.name: Ransomware Note File Creation (ransom.txt)
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Ransomware Note File Creation (ransom.txt)
id: 8b1c9c7a-4f5e-4b1d-8f9a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'ransom.txt', a common indicator of a ransomware infection.
references:
  - https://statescoop.com/water-treatment-plant-in-north-dakota-suffered-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\ransom.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a file named 'ransom.txt', which was found on the compromised SCADA server.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9c2d0d8b-5f6e-4c2e-9fa0-2c3d4e5f6a7b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://statescoop.com/water-treatment-plant-in-north-dakota-suffered-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete shadow copies, a technique associated with ransomware to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Suspicious RDP Traffic from IT to OT Network
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious RDP Traffic from IT to OT Network
id: a03e1e9c-6a7f-4d3f-a0b1-3d4e5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects RDP traffic from a corporate IT network to a sensitive OT/SCADA network, which could indicate lateral movement.
references:
  - https://statescoop.com/water-treatment-plant-in-north-dakota-suffered-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1021.001
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    destination.port: 3389
    source.ip_in: '192.168.0.0/16' # Placeholder for IT network range
    destination.ip_in: '10.0.0.0/8' # Placeholder for OT network range
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects RDP traffic from the IT network to the OT network, a potential vector for lateral movement in ICS environments.
